subject: Geology
title: Geology and Water
description: A 60-second lesson on Geology and Water.

hook.text: Two billion people depend on groundwater. Finding and protecting it is one of geology's most urgent tasks.

buildup.text: Hydrogeology studies how water moves through rock and soil, where it collects, and how fast it replenishes.

discovery.text: Aquifers are underground rock layers that store water. Some hold enough to supply cities for decades.

twist.text: Overuse can drain aquifers faster than rain refills them. Some take thousands of years to recover naturally.

climax.text: Water security is a geological problem. The rocks beneath us determine who has water and who doesn't.

punchline.text: The most precious resource hides underground.

quiz.question: What is an aquifer?
options: An underground rock layer that stores water
A fault line in the ocean
